What is a solar panel used for?

What does SSD mean for storage?
What device uses e-ink to display books?

What does QR code stand for?
What is a touchscreen?
easy.a: A display you control by touching it directly

What is a smart speaker?

What does an antivirus program protect against?
easy.a: Malware and viruses on your device

What is an app store?

medium.q: Who co-founded Google?
medium.a: Larry Page and Sergey Brin

medium.q: What chip architecture dominates smartphones?
medium.a: ARM architecture

medium.q: What is a neural network accelerator in phones called?
medium.a: A neural processing unit (NPU)

medium.q: Which Mac was the first to ship with Apple's M1 chip in November 2020?
medium.a: The MacBook Air

medium.q: What does LiDAR do on devices?
medium.a: Measures distance with lasers for depth mapping

medium.q: What is OLED's advantage over LCD?
medium.a: Self-lit pixels for better contrast and blacks

medium.q: What tech enables folding phones?
medium.a: Flexible OLED displays and hinge systems

medium.q: What is RISC-V?
medium.a: An open instruction set architecture

medium.q: What does SaaS stand for?
medium.a: Software as a Service

medium.q: What is edge computing?
medium.a: Processing data near the source instead of only in the cloud

medium.q: What battery chemistry powers most phones?
medium.a: Lithium-ion

medium.q: What is Wi-Fi 6 also known as?
What is Matter in smart homes?
medium.a: A unified connectivity standard for devices

medium.q: What is quantum computing's basic unit called?
medium.a: A qubit

medium.q: What is blockchain mainly used for?
medium.a: Decentralized ledgers like cryptocurrencies or supply tracking

medium.q: What does GPU acceleration help with beyond graphics?
medium.a: AI, simulations, and compute workloads

medium.q: What is a system-on-chip?
medium.a: Multiple components like CPU, GPU, modem on one chip

medium.q: What is a fabless chip company?
medium.a: Designing chips but outsourcing manufacturing

medium.q: What is battery energy density?
medium.a: Energy stored per unit mass or volume

medium.q: What is a heat pipe in laptops for?
medium.a: Moving heat away from processors to coolers

medium.q: What does UWB enable on devices?
medium.a: Precise short-range positioning

medium.q: What is FDM in 3D printing?
medium.a: Fused deposition modeling

medium.q: What is CNC machining used for in hardware?
medium.a: Computer-controlled cutting of parts

medium.q: What is an SoC die shrink?
medium.a: Moving to a smaller fabrication node for efficiency

medium.q: What does NVMe improve?
medium.a: Faster storage access over PCIe for SSDs

medium.q: What is Wi-Fi mesh backhaul?
medium.a: The wireless link between mesh nodes

medium.q: What is multi-factor authentication?
medium.a: Using two or more verification methods to log in

medium.q: What is zero trust in security?
medium.a: Never assume trust; always verify devices and users

medium.q: What is a PWA?
medium.a: A Progressive Web App

medium.q: What is serverless computing?
medium.a: Running code without managing servers, billed per execution

medium.q: What is haptic feedback?
medium.a: Tactile vibrations to simulate touch response

medium.q: What is optical image stabilization?
medium.a: Hardware that counters shake for clearer photos

medium.q: What is end-to-end encryption?
medium.a: Only senders and recipients can read the data

medium.q: What is containerization?
medium.a: Packaging software with dependencies to run consistently

medium.q: What is GraphQL used for?
medium.a: Querying APIs with flexible requests

medium.q: What type of chip uses light signals instead of electrical signals to move data?
medium.a: A photonic chip

medium.q: What is mmWave 5G known for?
medium.a: Very fast speeds but short range

medium.q: What is a privacy sandbox in browsers?
medium.a: APIs to reduce third-party tracking while allowing ads

medium.q: What is passkey login?
medium.a: Signing in using cryptographic keys stored on your device instead of a password

medium.q: What does Dolby Vision provide?
medium.a: Dynamic HDR metadata for better picture quality

medium.q: What is the main function of a TPU in Google's data centers?
medium.a: Accelerating machine learning workloads

medium.q: What was the name of IBM's chess-playing computer that defeated Garry Kasparov in 1997?
medium.a: Deep Blue

medium.q: What is the open-source Linux kernel's primary license called?
medium.a: GPL (GNU General Public License)

medium.q: What does USB stand for?
medium.a: Universal Serial Bus

medium.q: What company developed the Swift programming language?
medium.a: Apple

medium.q: What is Moore's Law?
medium.a: The observation that transistor counts on chips roughly double every two years

medium.q: What does DNS stand for on the internet?
medium.a: Domain Name System

medium.q: What protocol does the internet use to assign addresses to devices on a network?
medium.a: DHCP (Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol)

medium.q: What type of storage replaced spinning hard drives in most modern laptops by the 2010s?
medium.a: Solid-state drives (SSDs)

medium.q: What is an API?
medium.a: An Application Programming Interface — a way for software to communicate with other software

medium.q: What is the purpose of a CDN?
medium.a: Delivering web content from servers close to the user for faster load times

medium.q: What is the Git version control system primarily used for?
medium.a: Tracking changes in source code and coordinating collaborative software development

medium.q: What is a compiler?
medium.a: A program that translates source code into machine-executable code

medium.q: What year was the first iPhone released?
What company created the Kotlin programming language?
medium.a: JetBrains

hard.q: What TSMC process node was used to manufacture the Apple A17 Pro chip?
hard.a: TSMC's N3 (3 nm class) process

hard.q: What company pioneered EUV lithography tools?
hard.a: ASML

hard.q: What is chiplet architecture?
hard.a: Assembling a processor from multiple smaller dies interconnected in one package

hard.q: What is HBM memory used for?
hard.a: High-bandwidth memory stacked near processors

hard.q: What is PCIe 5.0's main benefit?
hard.a: Twice the throughput of PCIe 4.0

hard.q: What does ARMv9 add over ARMv8?
hard.a: Enhanced security and ML features

hard.q: What is ray tracing hardware for?
hard.a: Accelerating realistic light and shadow rendering

hard.q: What is tensor core hardware?
hard.a: Specialized units for matrix math in AI workloads

hard.q: What is chip binning?
hard.a: Sorting chips by performance and power characteristics

hard.q: What wavelength of light does extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography use to print chip patterns?
hard.a: 13.5 nanometers

hard.q: What packaging links dies with silicon interposers?
hard.a: 2.5D packaging

hard.q: What is Foveros?
hard.a: Intel's 3D chip stacking technology

hard.q: What is gate-all-around?
hard.a: A transistor design wrapping the gate around the channel

hard.q: What is MLOps?
hard.a: Practices for deploying and maintaining machine learning models

hard.q: What is a transformer model known for?
hard.a: Attention mechanisms for sequence tasks

hard.q: What is diffusion used for in generative AI?
hard.a: Iteratively denoising to create images or audio

hard.q: What does RISC stand for?
hard.a: Reduced Instruction Set Computer

hard.q: What is ARM's big.LITTLE design?
hard.a: Mixing high-performance and efficiency cores

hard.q: What is NVLink for?
hard.a: High-speed GPU-to-GPU interconnect

hard.q: What is CXL used for?
hard.a: Coherent interconnect for CPUs, GPUs, and memory pooling

hard.q: What is LPDDR5X known for?
hard.a: Low-power, fast mobile memory

hard.q: What is a TPM used for?
hard.a: Trusted Platform Module for secure keys and boot

hard.q: What is zero-knowledge proof technology for?
hard.a: Verifying information without revealing the data

hard.q: What is the role of a baseband modem?
hard.a: Handling radio communications and signal processing

hard.q: What is photonic interconnect research aiming at?
hard.a: Using light to move data between chips faster and cooler

hard.q: What is V2G in EVs?
hard.a: Vehicle-to-grid: bidirectional power flow allowing EVs to feed electricity back to the grid

hard.q: What is silicon anode research targeting?
hard.a: Higher capacity lithium batteries

hard.q: What is solid-state battery tech?
hard.a: Batteries using solid electrolytes for safety and density

hard.q: What does MIMO improve in wireless?
hard.a: Throughput and reliability using multiple antennas

hard.q: What is OFDM in 5G?
hard.a: Orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing modulation

hard.q: What is an AI accelerator card used for?
hard.a: Offloading machine learning inference or training

hard.q: What is wafer-scale integration?
hard.a: Using an entire silicon wafer as a single chip rather than cutting it into individual dies

hard.q: What is Open RAN?
hard.a: Disaggregated, interoperable radio access network architecture

hard.q: What is edge TPU hardware for?
hard.a: Running ML at the edge with low power

hard.q: What is Federated Learning?
hard.a: Training models across devices without centralizing data

hard.q: What is homomorphic encryption's promise?
hard.a: Computing on encrypted data without decrypting

hard.q: What is a microLED display?
hard.a: Self-emissive tiny LEDs for bright, efficient screens

hard.q: What does AV1 improve over H.264 and H.265?
hard.a: Better quality at the same bitrate, reducing bandwidth for streaming

hard.q: What is volumetric capture?
hard.a: Recording 3D scenes for immersive playback

hard.q: What is a digital twin used for?
hard.a: A virtual model of a physical system for simulation

hard.q: What is neuromorphic computing exploring?
hard.a: Brain-inspired hardware for efficient AI

hard.q: What interconnect standard replaced AGP for discrete graphics cards starting in 2004?
hard.a: PCI Express (PCIe)

hard.q: What does TSMC stand for?
hard.a: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company

hard.q: In what year did NVIDIA release its first GPU with dedicated ray tracing cores, the RTX 2000 series?
What is a FinFET transistor?
hard.a: A 3D fin-shaped transistor that reduces leakage current compared to planar designs

hard.q: What memory technology does Apple use in M-series chips that places RAM directly on the processor package?
hard.a: Unified Memory Architecture (UMA)

hard.q: What is the primary function of Arm's Cortex-X cores compared to its Cortex-A cores?
hard.a: Maximum single-thread peak performance at the cost of higher power

hard.q: What is NAND flash memory named after?
hard.a: The NAND logic gate structure used in its cell array

hard.q: What does QLC stand for in NAND flash?
hard.a: Quad-Level Cell — four bits stored per cell

hard.q: What company developed the CUDA parallel computing platform for GPU programming?
hard.a: NVIDIA

hard.q: What is the purpose of a hardware security enclave like Apple's Secure Enclave or ARM TrustZone?
hard.a: Isolating cryptographic operations and sensitive data from the main OS

hard.q: What is 3D NAND flash?
hard.a: Flash memory that stacks cell layers vertically to increase density without shrinking cell size

hard.q: What does the term 'dark silicon' describe in processor design?
hard.a: Transistors that must remain powered off to stay within thermal limits

hard.q: What is beamforming in Wi-Fi?
hard.a: Directing antenna signals toward a specific client for stronger, more targeted transmission

hard.q: What is the role of an IOMMU in a computer system?
hard.a: Mapping device memory accesses for security and virtualisation isolation
